items.title: US official: Collapse of Lebanon-Israel talks serves Hezbollah
items.desc: US reportedly asks Israel to avoid strikes in Beirut as official says collapse of Israel-Lebanon talks serves terror group.

items.title: Kim Jong-un Oversees Tests of AI-Guided Tactical Cruise Missile
items.desc: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un oversaw tests of major new weapons systems conducted by the DPRK Missile Administration and Academy of Defence Sciences on May 26, KCNA reported.

items.title: Trump's scorched-earth MAGA takedown jolts Republicans who fear November bloodbath: 'Rome is burning'
items.desc: President Donald Trump has officially sunk 'disloyal' Texas Senator John Cornyn, but this gambit to oust a the stalwart may cost Republicans down the line.

items.title: Ben-Gvir joins Border Police in upper Galilee, clashes with residents: 'We're the bosses'
items.desc: National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir toured anti-racketeering operations in Tuba-Zangariyye amid heated exchanges with residents.

items.title: Renewable energy is overtaking traditional power projects in Africa
items.desc: Africa’s energy sector is rapidly shifting toward solar and wind power and battery storage as governments and investors shift away from coal and large hydropower dams

items.title: Guide to the classics: Margaret Cavendish’s The Blazing World pioneered speculative fiction, 400 years ago
items.desc: The Blazing World is an amazing vision which ends as bewilderingly as it begins, making the reader’s head spin.

items.title: LEGO Foundation donates $97 million to bring play-based learning to more children in conflict zones
items.desc: The LEGO Foundation has committed $97 million to expand the International Rescue Committee's programs that use play to aid learning and recovery for children in conflict zones

items.title: Putin: Russia and Kazakhstan Are Allies in the Heart of Eurasia
items.desc: Ahead of his state visit to Kazakhstan, Russian President Vladimir Putin published an article titled “Russia–Kazakhstan: An Alliance in the Heart of Eurasia” in the Kazakhstanskaya Pravda newspaper, outlining key areas of bilateral partnership.

items.title: ICE detainees are dying by suicide at an 'alarming' rate, an AP investigation finds
items.desc: Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainees are taking their own lives at a pace that’s unprecedented in the agency’s two-decade history, highlighting what experts call failures in care and oversight

items.title: Rupee falls 8 paise to 95.78 against US dollar in early trade
items.desc: The rupee slipped 8 paise to 95.78 against US dollar in early trade on Wednesday on elevated crude oil prices as fresh US strikes dampened optimism for an imminent US-Iran peace deal.

items.title: S&P/ASX 200 Edges Higher to 8,669.9 as Modest Gains Lift Australian Shares Mid-Session
items.desc: The S&P/ASX 200 index rose slightly, reflecting cautious optimism amid global tensions and stable domestic economic indicators, with resource and financial stocks providing support despite broader market hesitation.
